title: Welcome To My Blog
text:
This is the first post in a Quarto blog. Welcome!

Since this post doesn’t specify an explicit image, the first image in the post will be used in the listing page of posts.

title: Collin Henkel
text:
I am a Data Science and Mathematics student at the University of Nebraska–Lincoln with experience in data analysis, SQL, and machine learning. My work focuses on building data-driven insights from structured datasets and developing reproducible analysis workflows.
This portfolio highlights selected projects involving statistical modeling, data validation, and applied machine learning.





Health Risk Modeling Analysis

Libraries: pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn

Methods: Regression, Classification, Statistical Inference

title: About
text:
About
I am a senior at the University of Nebraska–Lincoln studying Data Science and Mathematics. I am interested in data analysis, machine learning, and applying data-driven methods to real-world problems.
I have experience working with SQL, Python, and large structured datasets, including validating and analyzing production data systems.
I am currently seeking data analyst and data science roles.

title: Projects
text:
Built regression models to quantify relationships between socioeconomic factors, access, and health outcomes at the census tract level.
Tools: Python, pandas, scikit-learn, geopandas
Data: ACS, CDC PLACES, OpenStreetMap
View Report (PDF)
In Progress


See technical details


Built regression models and evaluated performance using R² and RMSE

Engineered features including distance to grocery stores and density metrics

Visualized spatial trends across geographic regions






Developed a structured business analysis combining data processing, visualization, and written insights to generate actionable recommendations.
Tools: Python, Quarto
View Project Site

Implemented and evaluated neural network models across text and image tasks, including sentiment analysis and image classification.
Tools: TensorFlow, Keras



Sentiment Analysis — sequential models for text classification

SVHN Classification — convolutional neural networks for digit recognition

Fashion MNIST — fully connected architectures with performance comparison

Built and trained neural network models across multiple datasets

Preprocessed text and image data for supervised learning tasks

Tuned architectures and hyperparameters to improve model performance
